CompanyProfile.title: CompanyProfile
CompanyProfile.description: Represents the core company's profile, including its headquarters location and default settings for travel time compensation.

id.description: Unique identifier for the CompanyProfile entity.

name.description: The official name of the company.

headquartersAddressStreet.description: The street address of the company's headquarters.

headquartersAddressCity.description: The city of the company's headquarters.

headquartersAddressPostalCode.description: The postal code of the company's headquarters.

headquartersAddressCountry.description: The country of the company's headquarters.

defaultTravelTimeThresholdMinutes.description: The default travel time in minutes beyond which extra compensation is applied (e.g., 60 minutes).

extraTravelTimeCompensationMinutes.description: The default extra travel time in minutes to compensate workers if the travel duration exceeds the threshold (e.g., 60 minutes).

Employee.title: Employee
Employee.description: Represents a user or worker within the company, with specific roles and reporting structures.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Employee entity.

companyId.description: Reference to the CompanyProfile this employee belongs to. (Relationship: CompanyProfile 1:N Employee)

firstName.description: The first name of the employee.

lastName.description: The last name of the employee.

email.description: The email address of the employee, used for communication.

role.description: The role of the employee within the company. (Enum: Admin, TeamLeader, Worker)

teamLeadId.description: Reference to the TeamLeader employee if this employee is a Worker. (Relationship: Employee (TeamLeader) 1:N Employee (Worker))

Client.title: Client
Client.description: Represents a customer or client for whom the company performs services.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Client entity.

companyId.description: Reference to the CompanyProfile this client is associated with. (Relationship: CompanyProfile 1:N Client)

name.description: The full name of the client or organization.

contactPerson.description: The primary contact person at the client's organization.

contactEmail.description: The email address of the client's contact person.

contactPhone.description: The phone number of the client's contact person.

JobSite.title: JobSite
JobSite.description: Represents a specific physical location where work is performed for a client.

id.description: Unique identifier for the JobSite entity.

clientId.description: Reference to the Client this job site belongs to. (Relationship: Client 1:N JobSite)

name.description: A descriptive name for the job site (e.g., 'Hauptstraße 120 - Berlin').

addressStreet.description: The street address of the job site.

addressCity.description: The city of the job site.

addressPostalCode.description: The postal code of the job site.

addressCountry.description: The country of the job site.

latitude.description: The geographical latitude of the job site for GPS calculations.

longitude.description: The geographical longitude of the job site for GPS calculations.

estimatedTravelTimeMinutesFromHQ.description: The estimated travel time in minutes from the company's headquarters to this job site, calculated by the mapping API.

TaskDefinition.title: TaskDefinition
TaskDefinition.description: Defines a standardized type of task or work that can be assigned (e.g., 'Garden Cleaning', 'Gate Maintenance').

id.description: Unique identifier for the TaskDefinition entity.

companyId.description: Reference to the CompanyProfile this task definition belongs to. (Relationship: CompanyProfile 1:N TaskDefinition)

name.description: The name of the task (e.g., 'Window Cleaning').

description.description: A detailed description of what the task entails.

MonthlyWorkPlan.title: MonthlyWorkPlan
MonthlyWorkPlan.description: Represents an overarching work schedule created for a specific month and year, containing multiple job assignments.

id.description: Unique identifier for the MonthlyWorkPlan entity.

companyId.description: Reference to the CompanyProfile this plan belongs to. (Relationship: CompanyProfile 1:N MonthlyWorkPlan)

title.description: A descriptive title for the monthly work plan (e.g., 'April 2024 Work Plan').

month.description: The month number for which the plan is created (1-12).

year.description: The year for which the plan is created.

createdByEmployeeId.description: Reference to the Employee (Admin or TeamLeader) who created this monthly plan. (Relationship: Employee 1:N MonthlyWorkPlan)

createdAt.description: The date and time when the monthly plan was created.

isPublished.description: Indicates whether the monthly plan has been published and is visible to workers.

JobAssignment.title: JobAssignment
JobAssignment.description: A specific planned job for an employee at a job site on a given date, forming part of a MonthlyWorkPlan.

id.description: Unique identifier for the JobAssignment entity.

monthlyWorkPlanId.description: Reference to the MonthlyWorkPlan this assignment belongs to. (Relationship: MonthlyWorkPlan 1:N JobAssignment)

assignedToEmployeeId.description: Reference to the Employee assigned to this job. (Relationship: Employee 1:N JobAssignment)

jobSiteId.description: Reference to the JobSite where the work is to be performed. (Relationship: JobSite 1:N JobAssignment)

scheduledStartDateTime.description: The planned date and time for the job assignment to start.

scheduledEndDateTime.description: The planned date and time for the job assignment to end.

taskDefinitionIds.description: References to the TaskDefinitions associated with this job assignment. (Relationship: TaskDefinition N:N JobAssignment)

notes.description: Additional notes or instructions from the manager/team leader for this assignment.

status.description: The current status of the job assignment. (Enum: Scheduled, InProgress, Completed, Canceled, Rescheduled)

isFarLocation.description: Indicates if the job site is considered a 'far location' based on travel time thresholds.

extraTravelTimeMinutesPlanned.description: The planned extra travel time in minutes to be compensated, automatically added if 'isFarLocation' is true.

TimeEntry.title: TimeEntry
TimeEntry.description: Records the actual clock-in and clock-out times, GPS locations, and calculated work/travel durations for a JobAssignment.

id.description: Unique identifier for the TimeEntry entity.

jobAssignmentId.description: Reference to the JobAssignment this time entry is for. (Relationship: JobAssignment 1:1 TimeEntry or 1:N if multiple clock-ins per assignment)

employeeId.description: Reference to the Employee who made this time entry. (Relationship: Employee 1:N TimeEntry)

clockInDateTime.description: The date and time when the employee clocked in for the job.

clockInLatitude.description: The geographical latitude recorded at clock-in.

clockInLongitude.description: The geographical longitude recorded at clock-in.

clockOutDateTime.description: The date and time when the employee clocked out from the job.

clockOutLatitude.description: The geographical latitude recorded at clock-out.

clockOutLongitude.description: The geographical longitude recorded at clock-out.

actualWorkMinutes.description: The actual duration in minutes the employee worked, calculated from clock-in and clock-out times.

actualTravelMinutes.description: The actual travel time in minutes, calculated by the app (e.g., from HQ to job site or based on route taken).

totalCompensatedMinutes.description: The total minutes to be compensated, including actual work minutes and any extra travel time compensation.

status.description: The approval status of the time entry. (Enum: Open, Submitted, Approved, Rejected)

submissionDateTime.description: The date and time when the time entry was submitted for approval.

approvedByEmployeeId.description: Reference to the Employee (TeamLeader or Admin) who approved this time entry. (Relationship: Employee 1:N TimeEntry)

approvalDateTime.description: The date and time when the time entry was approved.

teamLeaderNotes.description: Notes added by the Team Leader during the approval process.

WorkLogEntry.title: WorkLogEntry
WorkLogEntry.description: Stores evidence and notes provided by a worker for a specific time entry, such as photos or audio notes.

id.description: Unique identifier for the WorkLogEntry entity.

timeEntryId.description: Reference to the TimeEntry this log entry is associated with. (Relationship: TimeEntry 1:N WorkLogEntry)

employeeId.description: Reference to the Employee who created this log entry. (Relationship: Employee 1:N WorkLogEntry)

type.description: The type of log entry. (Enum: PhotoBefore, PhotoAfter, TextNote, AudioNote)

contentUrl.description: A URL pointing to the stored content (e.g., image file, audio file) if the type is Photo or AudioNote.

textNotes.description: Textual notes provided by the worker, if the type is TextNote.

createdAt.description: The date and time when the log entry was created.

definition.description: A minimal representation of the Employee entity, linking the Firebase `userId` (UID) to their `companyId` and `role`. This document is critical for fast authentication and authorization lookups across the application. The `userId` parameter represents the Firebase Authentication UID. Includes denormalized 'companyId' and 'role' for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The Firebase Authentication UID of the user.

definition.description: Stores the overarching profile and default settings for each company. The `companyId` parameter represents the unique identifier for a company. All other company-specific data is nested under this path.

params.name: companyId
params.description: The unique identifier for the company.

definition.description: Contains detailed profile information for each employee within a specific company. The `employeeId` parameter is the Firebase Authentication UID of the employee. Includes denormalized 'companyId' from path for authorization independence.

params.name: companyId
params.description: The unique identifier for the company.

params.name: employeeId
params.description: The unique identifier for the employee, which is their Firebase UID.

definition.description: Manages client accounts associated with a specific company. The `clientId` parameter represents the unique identifier for a client. Includes denormalized 'companyId' from path for authorization independence.

params.name: companyId
params.description: The unique identifier for the company.

params.name: clientId
params.description: The unique identifier for the client.

definition.description: Lists all physical job sites where work is performed for clients, organized by company. The `jobSiteId` parameter represents the unique identifier for a job site. Includes denormalized 'companyId' (from path) and 'clientId' for authorization independence.

params.name: companyId
params.description: The unique identifier for the company.

params.name: jobSiteId
params.description: The unique identifier for the job site.

definition.description: Defines standardized tasks available for use by a company. The `taskDefinitionId` parameter represents the unique identifier for a task definition. Includes denormalized 'companyId' from path for authorization independence.

params.name: companyId
params.description: The unique identifier for the company.

params.name: taskDefinitionId
params.description: The unique identifier for the task definition.

definition.description: Contains overarching monthly work schedules created by Admins/TeamLeaders for a company. The `monthlyWorkPlanId` parameter represents the unique identifier for a monthly work plan. Includes denormalized 'companyId' from path for authorization independence.

params.name: companyId
params.description: The unique identifier for the company.

params.name: monthlyWorkPlanId
params.description: The unique identifier for the monthly work plan.

definition.description: Represents specific job assignments, stored at the company level for ease of querying across all plans. The `jobAssignmentId` parameter represents the unique identifier for a job assignment. Includes denormalized 'companyId' (from path), 'monthlyWorkPlanId' (reference), 'assignedToEmployeeId' (reference), and 'isPlanPublished' (denormalized from MonthlyWorkPlan) for authorization independence.

params.name: companyId
params.description: The unique identifier for the company.

params.name: jobAssignmentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the job assignment.

definition.description: Records actual clock-in/out times and work durations for job assignments, stored at the company level for centralized reporting. The `timeEntryId` parameter represents the unique identifier for a time entry. Includes denormalized 'companyId' (from path), 'employeeId' (reference), 'jobAssignmentId' (reference), 'monthlyWorkPlanId' (denormalized from JobAssignment), and 'isPlanPublished' (denormalized from JobAssignment/MonthlyWorkPlan) for authorization independence.

params.name: companyId
params.description: The unique identifier for the company.

params.name: timeEntryId
params.description: The unique identifier for the time entry.

definition.description: Stores evidence and notes provided by workers for a specific time entry. The `workLogEntryId` parameter represents the unique identifier for a work log entry. Includes denormalized 'companyId' (from parent TimeEntry) and 'employeeId' (from parent TimeEntry) for authorization independence.

params.name: companyId
params.description: The unique identifier for the company.

params.name: timeEntryId
params.description: The unique identifier for the time entry.

params.name: workLogEntryId
params.description: The unique identifier for the work log entry.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is meticulously designed following strict security principles, prioritizing Authorization Independence and secure List queries (QAPs). The core strategy involves a multi-tenant model where all primary application data is segregated by company ID, residing under a top-level `/companies/{companyId}` collection. This ensures that data from one company is never inadvertently accessible by another. 

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL) & Denormalization:**
To eliminate the need for expensive and complex `get()` calls in Firestore Security Rules for authorization, key authorization contexts are denormalized directly into relevant documents. 

1.  **User Authentication Profile:** A dedicated `/users/{userId}` collection stores a minimal representation of the `Employee` entity for each Firebase `uid`. This document contains the `companyId` and `role` (Admin, TeamLeader, Worker) of the authenticated user. This allows security rules to quickly determine the user's context (`companyId` and `role`) with a single, direct read on `request.auth.uid`. This document is the bedrock of Authorization Independence, as its fields `companyId` and `role` are fundamental to all subsequent authorization checks.
2.  **Company ID Denormalization:** All application data documents belonging to a company (e.g., `JobSite`, `MonthlyWorkPlan`, `JobAssignment`, `TimeEntry`, `WorkLogEntry`) explicitly include their `companyId` field. This allows rules to check `resource.data.companyId == getUserCompanyId(request.auth.uid)` without navigating parent hierarchies.
3.  **Parent Context Denormalization:** For child or related documents whose access depends on parent or associated attributes, those attributes are denormalized. For example:
    *   `JobSite` documents include `clientId` and `companyId` (derived from its association).
    *   `JobAssignment` documents include `companyId`, `monthlyWorkPlanId`, `assignedToEmployeeId`, and crucially, `isPlanPublished` (denormalized from `MonthlyWorkPlan`). This means a worker's access to their assignments can be checked by comparing `request.auth.uid` with `assignedToEmployeeId` and ensuring `isPlanPublished` is true, without needing to `get()` the parent `MonthlyWorkPlan` document. This directly addresses the user's need for the 'plan to be saved in jobs' for easy handling.
    *   `TimeEntry` documents include `companyId`, `employeeId`, `jobAssignmentId`, `monthlyWorkPlanId`, and `isPlanPublished` (denormalized from `JobAssignment`). This allows validation of a time entry directly against the worker's UID and the plan's published status.
    *   `WorkLogEntry` documents include `companyId` and `employeeId` (denormalized from `TimeEntry`).

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters) & Structural Segregation:**
The structure explicitly supports Quality Assurance Protocols (QAPs) by enabling secure and efficient `list` operations without relying on security rules to filter results. This is achieved through:

1.  **Structural Segregation:** Data with different security postures or ownership are placed in distinct logical paths. All company-specific data is under `/companies/{companyId}`. Within this, `JobAssignment`s and `TimeEntry`s are stored in top-level collections under `/companies/{companyId}`, rather than deeply nested subcollections. This allows for flat, efficient queries across all jobs or time entries for a given company, which is crucial for management reporting and team overviews.
2.  **Field-Based Authorization:** For workers to view their specific assignments or time entries, they will perform queries like `db.collection('companies/{userCompanyId}/jobAssignments').where('assignedToEmployeeId', '==', request.auth.uid).where('isPlanPublished', '==', true)`. The denormalized fields (`assignedToEmployeeId`, `isPlanPublished`) ensure that the rule can directly validate the query's constraints against the document data, effectively enforcing QAPs. The security rules will then ensure that only documents matching these explicit `where` clauses are returned, combined with the user's `companyId` and `role` derived from the `/users/{userId}` profile. This enables robust and predictable query behavior, preventing unauthorized data from being returned.
